title: 10 best herbal creams for edema

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with edema because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort. The gel-like substance found in aloe vera has been shown to improve blood flow, which helps to dissipate excess fluid that accumulates in tissues, leading to edema. Additionally, aloe's antioxidant properties help to protect against damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy tissue repair and reducing inflammation. This makes it an effective natural remedy for relieving swollen ankles, feet, and hands associated with edema.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Aloe vera contains anti-inflammatory agents like aloin, aloe-emodin, and other polyphenols that reduce swelling by inhibiting prostaglandins and leukotrienes, which are chemicals that promote inflammation.

Moisturizing and soothing properties: The gel extracted from the aloe plant has moisturizing and soothing properties that help to reduce fluid retention and alleviate itching, redness, and discomfort associated with edema.

Antioxidant and free radical scavenging activity: Aloe vera's antioxidant properties help protect damaged tissue from further harm by neutralizing free radicals, which can contribute to inflammation and oxidative stress in conditions like edema.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, making them effective in reducing swelling and inflammation associated with edema.
Roots: Containing a unique combination of bioactive molecules that help to reduce fluid buildup and alleviate pain caused by edema.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 200g of fresh aloe vera gel and grind it into a smooth paste using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 50g of dried calendula flowers and 30g of dried witch hazel bark with the aloe vera paste.
Heat 100ml of sweet almond oil to 60°c and slowly add it to the mixture, stirring continuously for 10 minutes.
Add 20g of beeswax pellets and stir until melted, then allow the mixture to cool to 40°c.
Fill sterilized jars with the final product and store in a cool place for up to 6 months, allowing the cream to thicken and solidify fully.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with edema because they contain calendula, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that reduces swelling and relieves discomfort. Calendula's potent antioxidant properties also help to soothe and calm irritated skin, promoting healthy circulation and lymphatic drainage. As a result, pot marigold creams can effectively alleviate the symptoms of edema, such as puffiness, swelling, and discomfort, providing natural relief for individuals experiencing fluid retention.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and saponins, help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with edema.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold cream neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate inflammation and tissue damage caused by edema, promoting a faster recovery process.
Vasodilatory properties: Pot marigold's vasodilating compounds, such as flavonoids, help improve blood circulation, which reduces fluid buildup and swelling associated with edema.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce inflammation and relieve swelling associated with edema.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes of pot marigold have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat swelling and pain, making them a valuable addition to creams for edema.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers in late summer or early fall for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested flowers using a warm air circulation dryer at 35°c for 24 hours.
Combine 30g of dried calendula officinalis flowers with 100ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil and stir well.
Infuse the mixture in a dark glass container for 2 weeks, shaking daily, before straining through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle.
Mix the infused herbal extract with beeswax and vitamin e oil to create a stable emulsion cream at a temperature of 60°c using a double boiler setup.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mountain arnica creams helps with edema because of their unique ability to increase blood circulation and reduce inflammation. The herbal extracts in these creams, particularly arnica montana, have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate swelling and pain caused by fluid buildup. As the cream is applied topically, it stimulates the lymphatic system, promoting the removal of excess fluids and reducing edema. This natural remedy provides a safe and effective way to soothe and heal affected areas, providing relief from uncomfortable and painful symptoms associated with edema.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Arnica's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and ease pain associated with edema by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause inflammation.

Vasodilatory: Mountain arnica creams contain bioactive compounds that dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing fluid buildup in affected areas, thereby alleviating swelling and discomfort associated with edema.

Cellular detoxification: Arnica's saponins have been shown to stimulate the body's natural detoxification processes, helping to remove excess fluids and waste products from cells and tissues, which can contribute to the development of edema.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and triterpenoid saponins, roots are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them effective against swelling and pain associated with edema.
Rhyzomes: With its high content of beta-amyrin and oleanolic acid, rhyzomes possess potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, useful for reducing edema and promoting tissue repair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh arnica montana flowers and leaves, drying them for at least two weeks to preserve their potency.
Grind dried arnica montana flowers and leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Combine 250 grams of powdered arnica montana with 50 grams of beeswax and 100 grams of coconut oil, melting the mixture in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture for 30 minutes to ensure complete fusion of the ingredients, then remove from heat and let cool to room temperature.
Pour the cooled mixture into small containers and refrigerate for at least two hours to allow it to thicken and set before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with edema because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and alleviate pain. Ginger's bioactive molecules, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to inhibit prostaglandins, which are responsible for causing inflammation in the body. By reducing inflammation, herbal ginger creams can help to break down excess fluids and reduce swelling, providing relief from discomfort and stiffness associated with edema. Additionally, ginger's natural diuretic properties can also aid in removing excess fluids from the body, further contributing to its benefits in managing edema.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger contains anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate swelling associated with edema.
Vasodilatory effects: The volatile oils present in ginger have been shown to relax blood vessels and improve circulation, reducing fluid buildup and edema formation.
Lymphatic stimulation: Ginger's warming and stimulating properties can help stimulate the lymphatic system, promoting the removal of excess fluids and waste products that contribute to edema.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in bioactive compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease pain associated with edema.
Barks: Contain compounds like terpenes and phenolic acids, which exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, making them effective in treating edematous conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh ginger roots weighing approximately 100 grams for processing.
Dry the harvested ginger roots in a warm dark place for 7-10 days or until crispy brown.
Grind the dried ginger roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground ginger powder with sweet almond oil in a 1:2 ratio and vitamin e oil in a 1:1 ratio, stirring well for 30 minutes.
Transfer the mixture to sterilized glass containers, sealing tightly and allowing it to mature for 14-21 days before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with edema because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling in the body. The menthol present in peppermint cream has a natural ability to improve blood circulation and relieve pressure on veins and arteries, thereby reducing fluid accumulation and swelling. Additionally, peppermint's cooling effect can also help alleviate discomfort and pain associated with edema. By using herbal peppermint creams, individuals may experience relief from the discomfort and inflammation caused by edema.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Peppermint cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like menthol and camphor, which help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with edema.

Cooling and analgesic effects: The cooling sensation of peppermint cream helps numb the area, providing temporary relief from pain and discomfort caused by edema, while also reducing redness and warmth.

Diuretic properties: Peppermint's diuretic properties help increase urine production, which can aid in the removal of excess fluids that contribute to edema, reducing swelling and alleviating pressure on surrounding tissues.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain menthol, which helps to reduce inflammation and ease swelling associated with edema.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help soothe and calm swollen tissues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh peppermint leaves in early morning after dew has dried.
Chop the peppermint leaves into small pieces and air-dry them for at least 2 days.
Combine 100g of dried peppermint leaves with 200ml of carrier oil like coconut or olive oil.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily, to extract the active compounds.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and discard the solids, then add 10% beeswax and emulsify.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with edema because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The cream's active compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, work to reduce swelling by improving circulation and relaxing the blood vessels. Additionally, the cream's soothing and calming effects help to alleviate discomfort and stress, which can exacerbate edema. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, English lavender creams provide a natural and effective solution for managing edema and promoting overall well-being.

Antioxidant activity: Lavender's antioxidant properties help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to edema, and promote healthy circulation, reducing fluid retention and swelling.

Vasoconstrictive effect: The vasoconstrictive properties of lavender cream help constrict blood vessels, reducing blood flow to affected areas and thereby decreasing swelling and inflammation associated with edema.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots help reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation, making them an effective ingredient in creams for treating edema.
Seeds: Containing a high concentration of flavonoids and terpenes, the seeds contribute to the anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties of lavender-based creams for edema relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry high-quality lavandula angustifolia flowers to obtain the essential oil.
Combine dried lavender flowers with sweet almond oil in a 1:4 ratio by weight for infusion.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler for 2 hours at 160°f to extract the lavender essence.
Strain and filter the infused oil to remove the solid plant material and achieve a clear liquid consistency.
Mix the resulting herbal oil with beeswax and vitamin e oil in a ratio of 80:15:5 by weight and whip until smooth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort creams help with edema because of their ability to reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation. The herb's flavonoids and hyperforin have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate swelling, stiffness, and pain associated with edema. Additionally, St John's Wort's vasoactive compounds can dilate blood vessels, increasing blood flow to affected areas, which aids in the removal of excess fluid and reduces fluid buildup. This natural combination helps to soothe and calm edematous tissue, promoting a sense of comfort and relief.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: St. John's Wort creams contain antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to edema by reducing fluid accumulation in tissues.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in St. John's Wort creams, such as hyperforin and hypericin, help reduce swelling and pain associated with edema by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals.

Vasodilatory properties: The flavonoids present in St. John's Wort creams can dilate blood vessels, which helps improve circulation and reduces fluid buildup in tissues, thereby alleviating edema symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, such as hyperforin and hypericin, which exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective against edema.
Leaves: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, these have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help alleviate swelling and discomfort associated with edema.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100 grams of dried st. john's wort flowers hypericum perforatum and 50 grams of coconut oil.
Combine the dried st. john's wort flowers with 200 milliliters of vodka in a clean glass jar and allow it to steep for 14 days.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container discarding the solids and let it sit for 24 hours.
Mix 50 grams of beeswax with 100 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat until melted then add the strained liquid and stir well.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars and allow it to cool and set at room temperature before using.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with edema because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The curcumin present in turmeric has a powerful ability to reduce swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory molecules. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant and vasodilatory effects help to improve blood circulation, further reducing fluid accumulation and alleviating discomfort associated with edema. By applying topical turmeric creams directly to affected areas, individuals can experience relief from swelling and inflammation, promoting a faster recovery process.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: Curcumin, a bioactive compound found in turmeric, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with edema. This is because curcumin inhibits the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and free radicals that contribute to inflammation.

Corticosteroids: Turmeric creams may contain corticosteroid-like compounds that help reduce inflammation and swelling by decreasing the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that promote pain and swelling.

Antioxidants: The antioxidant properties of turmeric creams help protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to edema and other chronic inflammatory conditions. Antioxidants also help reduce inflammation and promote healthy tissue function.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes are a good source of curcuminoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and pain associated with edema.
Roots: Turmeric roots contain essential oils that help to reduce inflammation and improve circulation, making them useful in treating edema-related symptoms such as swelling and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 250g of coconut oil and heat it in a double boiler for 30 minutes.
Add 50g of beeswax pellets to the melted coconut oil and stir until dissolved.
Combine 100g of powdered turmeric curcuma longa root extract with 20ml of sweet almond oil in a small bowl.
Gradually add the turmeric mixture to the melted oil mixture stirring constantly for 10 minutes.
Pour the final mixture into sterilized containers and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower creams helps with edema because they contain a unique combination of anti-inflammatory compounds, including echinacea, which has been shown to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort associated with fluid retention. The cream's soothing properties also help to calm irritated skin, while its antioxidant-rich ingredients work to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate inflammation. By targeting the root causes of edema, these creams provide effective relief for those experiencing water retention.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Purple coneflower creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like quercetin, kaempferol, and isorhapontigenin, which help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with edema.

Antioxidant properties: The herbal extracts in purple coneflower creams possess antioxidant properties, which neutralize free radicals that can contribute to tissue damage and fluid buildup leading to edema.

Vasodilatory effects: Purple coneflower creams contain vasodilators like flavonoids, which help dilate blood vessels, improving circulation and reducing swelling by allowing excess fluid to be removed from the affected area more efficiently.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds like triterpenoids and phenolic acids, which can help reduce swelling and ease discomfort associated with edema.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes have been traditionally used to treat water retention and swelling due to their diuretic properties and ability to improve lymphatic circulation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry high-quality echinacea purpurea flowers according to botanical standards for at least two weeks.
Combine one part dried echinacea purpurea flowers with three parts cold-pressed coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six weeks allowing the herbs to infuse into the oil.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container discarding the solids and retaining the infused oil.
Add one tablespoon of beeswax and one teaspoon of vitamin e oil to the infused oil blending until smooth and creamy for a final preparation time of five minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with edema because they contain mucilages, which are thick, protective substances that can help to reduce swelling. The marshmallow root has anti-inflammatory properties that can ease pain and discomfort associated with edema, while also promoting healthy circulation. Additionally, the creamy texture of herbal marshmallow creams can provide a soothing and comforting sensation on affected areas, further aiding in the relief of symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The flavonoids and polyphenols present in marshmallow creams have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the production of pro-inflammatory mediators that contribute to edema.

Wound-healing properties: The antioxidant and antimicrobial compounds found in marshmallow creams promote wound healing by enhancing tissue repair, reducing the risk of infection, and improving the overall healing process.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps soothe and calm skin irritations associated with edema.
Barks: The barks contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and alleviate symptoms of edema.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 grams of dried marshmallow root althaea officinalis flowers or leaves.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine 20 grams of the ground marshmallow root powder with 100 grams of sweet almond oil and 50 grams of beeswax in a heat-proof bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat stirring occasionally for 10-15 minutes until the beeswax melts fully.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken at room temperature for 30-60 minutes before transferring it to glass jars.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm creams helps with edema because of its unique ability to soothe and calm irritated skin tissue. The mucilages present in slippery elm work as a protective barrier, reducing inflammation and swelling by locking in moisture and reducing the appearance of puffiness. Additionally, slippery elm's anti-inflammatory properties help to constrict blood vessels, further alleviating fluid retention and promoting healthy circulation, ultimately leading to a reduction in edema symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory: The herbal cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like salicin and taraxasterol, which help reduce swelling and alleviate pain caused by edema.
Astringent: Slippery elm's astringent properties help to constrict blood vessels and reduce fluid buildup in the affected area, thereby reducing edema.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of slippery elm are rich in mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps to soothe and reduce inflammation in skin affected by edema.
Barks: Slippery elm barks contain compounds like salicin and gallic acid, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of edema.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ulmus rubra bark and dry it properly according to traditional methods for two weeks.
Grind 20 grams of dried ulmus rubra bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for five minutes.
Mix the powdered ulmus rubra bark with 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil and 50 milliliters of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over medium heat for one hour, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture reaches a temperature of 160 degrees fahrenheit.
Remove the double boiler from the heat and let it cool to room temperature, then strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and store it in an amber glass jar at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams help with edema because of their anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort. The soothing and calming effects of chamomile also promote relaxation, allowing for improved circulation and reduced fluid retention. remedy_parts: Roots: Chamomile roots are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with edema.
Flowers: The flowers of the chamomile plant contain apigenin, an anti-inflammatory compound that helps alleviate symptoms of edema.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 200g of beeswax and melt it in a double boiler at low heat for 30 minutes.
Combine 300ml of distilled water with 20g of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the herb infusion through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container discarding solids.
Mix the melted beeswax with 100ml of sweet almond oil and 60ml of coconut oil until well combined.
Gradually add the cooled herbal infusion to the oil mixture while whipping with an electric mixer for 10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary creams helps with edema because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort. The essential oils present in rosemary have been shown to improve circulation, helping to drain excess fluid from affected areas. Additionally, rosemary's antioxidant properties help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy tissue repair and reducing inflammation. As a result, applying rosemary creams to areas affected by edema can provide relief and promote faster healing.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as camphor and bornyl acetate, help reduce swelling and ease pain associated with edema.
Vasodilatory properties: The essential oil of rosemary, borneol, causes blood vessels to dilate, which improves circulation and reduces fluid accumulation in tissues, alleviating edema symptoms.
Antioxidant properties: Rosemary's antioxidants help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to edema, while also promoting tissue repair and reducing scarring.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and improve circulation, making them effective in relieving edema.
Barks: The bark of the rosemary plant contains flavonoids and terpenes that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of edema.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves and chop them into fine pieces for infusion.
Combine the chopped herbs with 400 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-resistant container and steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the herbal mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bowl, discarding solids, and let it cool to room temperature.
Mix the cooled herb-infused liquid with 100 grams of cold-pressed sweet almond oil, 50 grams of beeswax, and 20 milliliters of glycerin in a double boiler.
Continue heating the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted and the cream has a smooth consistency, taking about 30 minutes to prepare.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Geranium creams helps with edema because of their unique ability to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation. The herbs in geranium, such as pelargonium graveolens, contain bioactive compounds that stimulate the lymphatic system and promote the removal of excess fluids from tissues. This helps to alleviate swelling and discomfort associated with edema. Additionally, geranium creams have a cooling effect on the skin, which can also help to reduce inflammation and ease pain. remedy_properties: Diuretic properties: Geranium cream helps to reduce fluid retention by stimulating the kidneys to eliminate excess water, thereby alleviating swelling and discomfort.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh pelargonium graveolens leaves and stems, ensuring 50 grams of plant material for the recipe.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator at 40°c for 4 hours or by air drying for 7-10 days.
Combine 20 grams of dried pelargonium graveolens with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil and let it steep for 2 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and mix it with 30% beeswax, 15% shea butter, and 1 tablespoon of vitamin e oil to create a cream base.
Heat the mixture at 60°c for 10 minutes while stirring until the wax is fully incorporated and the mixture has cooled to form a smooth, creamy consistency suitable for use on the skin.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with edema because they contain a unique combination of compounds that target the underlying causes of swelling. remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh ginkgo biloba leaves from a trusted source and wash them thoroughly with distilled water.
Dry the ginkgo biloba leaves in a single layer at room temperature for 7-10 days or until crisp and brittle.
Combine 50 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks in a cool dark place, shaking the jar daily to ensure even extraction of active compounds.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container and discard the solids after 30 minutes of preparation time.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh glycyrrhiza glabra roots, dry them in shade for 14 days to preserve bioactive compounds.
Grind 50 grams of dried roots into fine powder using mortar and pestle within 30 minutes.
Combine powdered root with 200 milliliters of distilled water, simmer mixture at 70 degrees celsius for 20 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discard solids after 10 minutes.
Mix 100 grams of petroleum jelly with 50 grams of cooled plant extract, whip until smooth and creamy in 15 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest sambucus nigra flowers and leaves, ensure freshness and dry thoroughly for later use.
Combine one part dried sambucus nigra flowers and two parts olive oil in a heat-proof glass bowl.
Place the mixture in a double boiler or a saucepan set over simmering water, stir occasionally for 4-6 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container, discard solids.
Allow the infused oil to cool and thicken for at least 24 hours before whipping with beeswax and applying as needed.

Vasodilatory: Lemon balm's vasodilatory effects help to dilate blood vessels, improving circulation and reducing pressure on surrounding tissues, which can contribute to edema formation.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of lemon balm cream neutralize free radicals that can damage tissue and contribute to the development of edema, promoting healthy skin and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon balm roots contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease discomfort associated with edema.

Leaves: The leaves of the lemon balm plant are rich in essential oils, such as citral and geraniol, which have natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, making them effective in soothing edematous skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves at dawn on a dry day weighing 20% of the total cream's weight.
Dry the leaves in a warm dark place for 7-10 days or by using a food dehydrator at 60 degrees celsius.
Mix 10 grams of dried melissa officinalis leaves with 100 grams of sweet almond oil and 50 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture to 65 degrees celsius and stir until the beeswax is fully incorporated and melted after 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into glass jars and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature within 2 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow creams helps with edema because of its unique ability to reduce inflammation and promote lymphatic circulation. The active compounds in yarrow, such as acetic acid and chamazulene, have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate swelling and discomfort associated with edema. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties help improve skin elasticity and tone, reducing the appearance of puffy skin. By supporting healthy circulation and reducing inflammation, herbal yarrow creams provide a natural and effective solution for relieving edema-related discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Yarrow creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds such as sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, which help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with edema.

Vasoconstrictors: The herbal extracts in yarrow cream contain vasoconstrictors that help narrow blood vessels and restrict blood flow to the affected area, reducing fluid accumulation and alleviating edema.

Antioxidants: Yarrow creams often include antioxidant-rich herbs like chamomile or calendula, which help neutralize free radicals and prevent oxidative damage to skin tissues, thereby promoting healthy skin function and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the yarrow plant are rich in flavonoids and polyphenols, which help to reduce inflammation and swelling associated with edema.
Leaves: Yarrow leaves contain sesquiterpenes and lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and relieving pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried achillea millefolium flowers at the rate of one part flower to two parts carrier oil.
Infuse the dried flowers in sweet almond oil for four hours using a double boiler method.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass container, retaining 250 milliliters of the extract.
Combine the retained extract with beeswax and coconut oil at a ratio of 1:1:2 and heat gently until melted.
Pour the mixture into sterilized jars and allow it to cool and solidify for two hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with edema because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and promote lymphatic drainage. The cream's diuretic properties also help to eliminate excess fluids from the body, reducing fluid buildup in tissues and organs. Additionally, dandelion's flavonoids and terpenes have been shown to improve circulation, further aiding in the reduction of edema. By addressing the underlying causes of fluid accumulation, dandelion creams provide natural relief for individuals suffering from this common condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol and taraxetin, reduce swelling and pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.
Diuretic properties: The diuretic effects of dandelion extract in creams increase urine production, which helps to flush out excess fluids and reduce edema in the body.

Antioxidant properties: Dandelion cream's antioxidant compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, protect tissues from oxidative damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy blood vessels and reducing fluid buildup.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making it effective in reducing swelling and inflammation associated with edema.
Rhyzomes: Containing bioactive compounds like taraxasterol, which has been shown to possess anti-edematous properties and help alleviate symptoms of edema.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh dandelion roots and chop them into small pieces.
Mix the chopped roots with 200ml of boiling water for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to extract the liquid.
Combine the extracted liquid with 50g of beeswax, 20g of coconut oil, and 10g of shea butter, heating the mixture until melted and well combined.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for edema are topical treatments that utilize natural herbs and plant extracts to alleviate swelling, pain, and discomfort associated with edema. These creams work by reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and promoting lymphatic drainage, which helps to reduce fluid buildup in the body. Examples of herbal creams that help with edema include creams containing arnica, peppermint, and witch hazel, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. By using these creams, individuals suffering from edema can experience reduced swelling, improved mobility, and enhanced quality of life, allowing them to perform daily activities with greater ease and comfort.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat edema with herbal creams is to choose products containing anti-inflammatory herbs like arnica, calendula, and St. John's Wort. These ingredients help reduce swelling and promote blood flow. Look for creams that also contain soothing herbs like aloe vera and chamomile to calm irritated skin. Apply the cream topically 2-3 times a day, gently massaging into the affected area until fully absorbed.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to edema that are treated with herbal creams are skin conditions characterized by inflammation, swelling, and fluid retention. Herbal creams used to treat these ailments often contain anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Examples of such conditions include eczema, psoriasis, rosacea, and cellulite. These creams can be made from natural ingredients like aloe vera, tea tree oil, chamomile, and arnica, which help soothe and calm the skin while reducing inflammation and promoting collagen production.
